title: fagara mantchurica f. angustifolia
intro: Fagara mantchurica f. angustifolia, also known as Chinese prickly ash, is a type of plant in the Rutaceae family. It is a shrub or small tree native to China and Korea, and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots contain compounds with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including pain relief, fever reduction, and immune system support. Fagara mantchurica f. angustifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
